rulings.Case Title: KESHA CROMARTIE VS. NORTHRIDGE COOPERATIVE HOMES,INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO AMEND And Notice Of Motion; Declaration Of Kelsey Merrick
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 22, 2024 line 1. PETITIONER KESHA CROMARTIE'S MOTION TO AMEND AND NOTICE OF MOTION is GRANTED. The Court finds no prejudice to Respondents. Petitioner to file First Amended Petition for Writ of Mandate and Complaint within five (5) court days. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MALCOLM DRILLING COMPANY, INC. VS. SWINERTON WEBCOR ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 22, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT SWINERTON WEBCOR'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-20-588224, CGC-20-588256, CGC-21-590207, CGC-21-590929, CGC-21-591132, CGC-21-591196, CGC-21-591319, CGC-21-591783, CGC-21-593176, CGC-21-594098, CGC-22-598031, CGC-22-599444, CGC-22-600848 WITH CASE CGC-20-586851 AND LIFT STAYS is continued to January 29, 2024, to allow counsel to file substitution of attorney or change of address for all cases that are subject to the arbitration agreement. The Court has reviewed the record for all cases proposed for consolidation and it appears that not all parties' counsel of record was served. For example: in CGC-20-586851, Malcolm Drilling Company, Inc. v. Swinerton Webcor, et al., Defendant Oceanwide Center, LLC's Attorney of Record is Anthony Barron of Nixon Peabody LLP, but the Proof of Service indicates that John Foust of Ralls Gruber & Niece LLP was served. Further, Plaintiff Malcolm Drilling Company, Inc.'s Attorney of Record is David Weaver of Oles Morrison Rinker & Baker, LLP, but David Weaver of Smith, Currie & Hancock LLP was served. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GERARDO MARTIN SANCHEZ ET AL VS. WHITE CAP CONSTRUCTION SUPPLY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Granting Leave To File Amended Answer
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 22, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT WHITE CAP CONSTRUCTION SUPPLY, INC.'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED ANSWER is GRANTED, in part, and DENIED, in part. Defendant's Motion is GRANTED as to Defendant's Thirty-Eighth Affirmative Defense (Worker's Compensation, Witt v. Jackson). Defendant's Motion is DENIED as to all other affirmative defenses, pursuant to stipulation on file. Defendant to file amended answer within five (5) court days. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: BRETT MILLIER, AS SUCCESSOR CO-TRUSTEE OF THE ET AL VS. MARC DANA MERKER, AN INDIVIDUAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT FOR PARTITION BY SALE OF REAL PROPERTY, FOR APPOINTMENT OF PARTITION REFEREE, FOR TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER, AND FOR OTHER RELIEF
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 22, 2024 line 4. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT FOR PARTITION BY SALE OF REAL PROPERTY, FOR APPOINTMENT OF PARTITION REFEREE, FOR TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER, AND FOR OTHER RELIEF is GRANTED with modifications. Any refences to the payment of $305,113.84 and "encumbering, assigning, transferring or selling his interest in the Subject Property" shall be removed from the proposed orders submitted on January 10, 2024. Plaintiff shall prepare proposed orders in conformity with this ruling and present them to the Court for execution. The Court notes that Plaintiff failed to comply with the express instructions regarding the contents of the supplemental briefing, the following parts of the supplemental briefing are stricken: page 2:1-page 3:16; page 5:9-17. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). DEFENDANT EQUITY GROWTH ASSET MANAGEMENT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend the deficiencies that the Demurrer addressed.
